extensionName.description: Name shown in the browser and the store.

extensionDescription.message: Krafty is HTML Checker for Web Director
extensionDescription.description: Short description shown in the browser and the store.

nestReasonNotAllowed.description: Tooltip on an element its parent may not contain.

nestReasonEmptyModel.description: Tooltip when the parent is a void or text-only element.

nestAllowedChildren.description: Second tooltip line listing what the parent does allow.

nestPanelClean.description: Shown when the page has no findings.

nestPanelCountOne.description: Total when there is exactly one finding.

panelScannedAt.message: Scanned $time$. Elements added later are not included.
panelScannedAt.description: Reminder that the scan is a snapshot of the DOM.

panelClose.description: Label of the button that dismisses a panel.

checkerOutline.description: Menu label. The five that build a panel take their name from the panel's own title key, so the menu and the panel cannot disagree; these three have no panel.

checkerAlt.description: Menu label for the checker that draws every image's alt text.

checkerBrightness.description: Menu label for the checker that greys the page.

popupGroupReports.description: Heading over the checkers that open a panel and say what they found.

popupGroupOverlays.description: Heading over the checkers that draw over the page instead of reporting.

popupReview.description: Runs every checker that reports and puts all of their findings on the clipboard.

panelRescan.description: Runs the check over the page as it is now, for content a single page app added after the first scan.

checkerHead.description: Heading of the head metadata panel.

sectionChecks.description: Heading of the section listing mechanical findings. Shared by every panel that reports findings.

sectionReview.description: Heading of the section holding what only a person can judge.

headSectionReference.description: Heading of the section listing every tag.

checksClean.message: Nothing wrong in what can be checked automatically.
checksClean.description: Deliberately narrow: presence and shape were checked, not whether the content is correct.

checksCountOne.description: Used when there is exactly one mechanical finding.

headReviewNote.message: Whether this is the right text cannot be decided automatically. Read it as a visitor would see it.

headPreviewSearch.description: Label above the search result preview.

headPreviewCard.description: Label above the social card preview.

headPreviewApproximate.message: Approximate. Search engines rewrite these often, and cut them off by width rather than by character count.
headPreviewApproximate.description: Honesty note: the previews are not a promise of how anything will actually render.

headCardFallbackTitle.message: no og:title, falling back to title
headCardFallbackTitle.description: Noted on the card preview when og:title is absent.

headCardFallbackDescription.message: no og:description, falling back to description
headCardFallbackDescription.description: Noted on the card preview when og:description is absent.

headCardNoImage.description: Shown in place of the card image when og:image is absent.

checkerMarkup.description: Heading of the markup panel. Named for the family, not for the one check it starts with.

markupSectionIds.description: Heading of the section listing duplicated ids.

markupIdListLabel.description: Label above the list of duplicated ids.

markupDuplicateId.message: $count$ ids are used more than once
markupDuplicateId.description: A duplicated id breaks label-for, in-page anchors, aria-labelledby and any script asking for the element.

markupDuplicateIdOne.message: 1 id is used more than once
markupDuplicateIdOne.description: Used when exactly one id is duplicated.

markupTableNoHeader.message: $count$ tables have no header cells, so every cell is announced bare
markupTableNoHeader.description: A th is what a screen reader announces a cell along with. Layout tables marked role=presentation are excluded.

markupTableNoHeaderOne.message: 1 table has no header cells, so every cell is announced bare
markupTableNoHeaderOne.description: Used when exactly one table is missing its headers.

markupSectionFields.description: Heading of the section listing form fields with no name.

markupFieldListLabel.description: Label above the list of unlabelled form fields. Each row is the field, with its placeholder beside it where it has one.

markupSectionSvg.description: Heading of the section listing SVGs that are neither named nor hidden.

markupSvgListLabel.description: Label above the list of SVGs that are neither named nor hidden.

markupInputNoLabel.message: $count$ form fields have nothing naming them
markupInputNoLabel.description: No label for, no wrapping label, no aria-label, no aria-labelledby that resolves, and no title. Hidden fields and the button types are not counted.

markupInputNoLabelOne.message: 1 form field has nothing naming it
markupInputNoLabelOne.description: Used when exactly one field is unlabelled.

markupSvgNoName.message: $count$ inline SVGs are neither named nor hidden, so how they are announced depends on the screen reader
markupSvgNoName.description: No title, aria-label, aria-labelledby or role=img to name it, and no aria-hidden on it or above it to skip it.

markupSvgNoNameOne.message: 1 inline SVG is neither named nor hidden, so how it is announced depends on the screen reader
markupSvgNoNameOne.description: Used when exactly one SVG is neither named nor hidden.

markupSectionLinks.description: Heading of the section listing links and buttons with no accessible name.

markupLinkListLabel.description: Label above the list of links and buttons with no accessible name. A link's address is shown beside it.

markupLinkNoName.message: $count$ links or buttons have no accessible name, so a screen reader announces only their role
markupLinkNoName.description: No text, no aria-label, no aria-labelledby that resolves, no alt on an image inside, and no title. The icon-only button is the usual case.

markupLinkNoNameOne.message: 1 link or button has no accessible name, so a screen reader announces only its role
markupLinkNoNameOne.description: Used when exactly one link or button has no accessible name.

markupSvgUnnamedControl.message: $count$ inline SVGs are the only content of a link or button, leaving it unnamed
markupSvgUnnamedControl.description: The heavier half of the SVG finding: the svg is the reason its containing link or button has no accessible name.

markupSvgUnnamedControlOne.message: 1 inline SVG is the only content of a link or button, leaving it unnamed
markupSvgUnnamedControlOne.description: Used when exactly one SVG leaves its control unnamed.

markupSvgControlTag.description: Aside on an SVG row that is the reason its link or button has no accessible name.

markupLinkDead.message: $count$ links point nowhere — their href is empty
markupLinkDead.description: A link whose href is empty reloads the page it is on. A bare # is left alone: it cannot be told from a script-driven control in static markup. A fragment like #main is a real in-page jump and is not counted.

markupLinkDeadOne.message: 1 link points nowhere — its href is empty
markupLinkDeadOne.description: Used when exactly one link points nowhere.

markupSectionDeadLinks.description: Heading of the section listing links whose href is empty or a bare #.

markupDeadLinkListLabel.description: Label above the list of dead links. Each row is the link, with its text beside it.

markupLinkSameText.message: $count$ link texts each lead to more than one place, so a link list repeats a name that goes somewhere different
markupLinkSameText.description: One accessible name pointing at several distinct addresses. A screen reader user navigating by link cannot tell them apart.

markupLinkSameTextOne.message: 1 link text leads to more than one place, so a link list repeats a name that goes somewhere different
markupLinkSameTextOne.description: Used when exactly one link text is reused for different destinations.

markupSectionReused.description: Heading of the section listing link texts used for more than one destination.

markupReusedListLabel.description: Label above the list of reused link texts. Each row is the text, with how many destinations it has.

markupSectionVague.description: Heading of the section listing link texts that are vague out of context. Listed, not asserted to be wrong.

markupVagueListLabel.message: Vague on their own — you decide
markupVagueListLabel.description: Label above the list of vague link texts. Whether a phrase is too vague is a judgement, so these are listed for the reader.

markupRoleNotFocusable.message: $count$ elements have an interactive role but cannot take focus, so the control they promise cannot be reached
markupRoleNotFocusable.description: role=button, link, checkbox and the like on an element that is neither natively focusable nor given a tabindex. Composite roles managed by a roving tabindex are not counted.

markupRoleNotFocusableOne.message: 1 element has an interactive role but cannot take focus, so the control it promises cannot be reached
markupRoleNotFocusableOne.description: Used when exactly one element has an interactive role it cannot be focused to use.

markupSectionRoleFocus.description: Heading of the section listing elements with an interactive role that cannot take focus.

markupRoleFocusListLabel.message: A role, but no way to focus it
markupRoleFocusListLabel.description: Label above the list. Each row is the element, with its role beside it.

markupHiddenFocusable.message: $count$ aria-hidden elements are still in the tab order, so focus lands on something announced as nothing
markupHiddenFocusable.description: aria-hidden=true on, or wrapping, an element a keyboard can focus. Removed from the accessibility tree but left tabbable.

markupHiddenFocusableOne.message: 1 aria-hidden element is still in the tab order, so focus lands on something announced as nothing
markupHiddenFocusableOne.description: Used when exactly one aria-hidden element is still focusable.

markupSectionHiddenFocus.description: Heading of the section listing aria-hidden elements that are still focusable.

markupHiddenFocusListLabel.description: Label above the list of aria-hidden elements left in the tab order.

markupTabindexPositive.message: $count$ elements have a tabindex above zero, which reorders the whole document's tab sequence
markupTabindexPositive.description: A positive tabindex pulls the element to the front of the document tab order rather than the local place it looks like it sits in.

markupTabindexPositiveOne.message: 1 element has a tabindex above zero, which reorders the whole document's tab sequence
markupTabindexPositiveOne.description: Used when exactly one element has a positive tabindex.

markupSectionTabindex.description: Heading of the section listing elements with a tabindex above zero.

markupTabindexListLabel.description: Label above the list of elements with a positive tabindex. Each row shows the value.

checkerImage.description: Heading of the image weight panel.

imageSectionList.description: Heading of the section listing the oversized images.

imageListLabel.description: Label above the list of oversized images.

imageOversized.message: $count$ images are served much larger than they are shown
imageOversized.description: Wasted bytes: the browser downloads pixels it then throws away.

imageOversizedOne.message: 1 image is served much larger than it is shown
imageOversizedOne.description: Used when exactly one image is oversized.

imageNoSize.message: $count$ images have no width and height: the layout jumps as they load
imageNoSize.description: Without the attributes the browser cannot reserve the space.

imageNoSizeOne.message: 1 image has no width and height: the layout jumps as it loads
imageNoSizeOne.description: Used when exactly one image is missing its dimensions.

imageSizeComparison.description: The file's own size against the size it is displayed at.

imageAllowanceNote.message: Measured against $allowance$× the displayed size, so an image built for a high-density display is not reported. The ratio on each row is how far past that it goes.
imageAllowanceNote.description: States the basis, because the threshold is conventional rather than measured.

imageUnmeasured.message: $count$ images could not be measured, having not finished loading. Scroll them into view and run the check again.
imageUnmeasured.description: Lazy loaded images below the fold have no natural size yet.

imageUnmeasuredOne.message: 1 image could not be measured, having not finished loading. Scroll it into view and run the check again.
imageUnmeasuredOne.description: Used when exactly one image was not measurable.

checkerLeftovers.description: Heading of the leftovers panel, and its menu label. Development leftovers a page shipped with.

leftoversLocal.message: $count$ resources point at a local or private address no visitor can reach
leftoversLocal.description: localhost, 127.x, a 192.168 or 10.x host, a .local name, in a src, href, srcset or action. The page's own host is not counted.

leftoversLocalOne.message: 1 resource points at a local or private address no visitor can reach
leftoversLocalOne.description: Used when exactly one resource points at a local or private address.

leftoversMixed.message: $count$ resources load over http on an https page, so the browser blocks them
leftoversMixed.description: Mixed content: an http resource on an https page. Fully decidable and already broken.

leftoversMixedOne.message: 1 resource loads over http on an https page, so the browser blocks it
leftoversMixedOne.description: Used when exactly one resource is mixed content.

leftoversPlaceholder.message: $count$ images still come from a placeholder service
leftoversPlaceholder.description: placehold.co, dummyimage.com and the like: an image nobody swapped for the real one. A closed list.

leftoversPlaceholderOne.message: 1 image still comes from a placeholder service
leftoversPlaceholderOne.description: Used when exactly one image is from a placeholder service.

leftoversSectionLocal.description: Heading of the section listing resources on a local or private host.

leftoversLocalListLabel.description: Label above the list of local and private addresses. Each row is the address, with the element's tag.

leftoversSectionMixed.description: Heading of the section listing http resources on an https page.

leftoversMixedListLabel.description: Label above the list of mixed-content resources.

leftoversSectionPlaceholder.description: Heading of the section listing images from a placeholder service.

leftoversPlaceholderListLabel.description: Label above the list of placeholder images.

checkerHeading.description: Heading of the heading structure panel.

headingReviewNote.message: Whether these read as a sensible outline of the page cannot be decided automatically. Read them in order, as a table of contents.
headingReviewNote.description: Explains why the outline is shown rather than judged.

headingOutlineLabel.description: Label above the indented list of headings.

headingNoneFound.description: Neither h1-h6 nor role=heading was found.

headingNoH1.message: No level 1 heading: nothing names the page as a whole
headingNoH1.description: The outline starts below the top level.

headingMultipleH1.message: $count$ level 1 headings: only one should name the page
headingMultipleH1.description: Allowed by the spec, but in practice one h1 names the page and the rest are a mistake.

headingSkipped.message: $from$ is followed by $to$, skipping a level
headingSkipped.description: A jump down the outline leaves a gap with nothing in it.

headingEmpty.description: An empty heading announces nothing. Image-only headings count as having text, via the alt.

headingHiddenOne.message: 1 heading is hidden from everyone and was left out.
headingHiddenOne.description: Used when exactly one heading was left out.

headingHidden.message: $count$ headings are hidden from everyone and were left out.
headingHidden.description: display:none, visibility:hidden and aria-hidden take a heading out of the accessibility tree. Visually hidden headings are not affected and are included.

checkerLandmark.description: Heading of the landmark regions panel.

landmarkReviewNote.message: Whether the page marks up the regions it ought to cannot be decided automatically. Read them in order, as a map of the page.
landmarkReviewNote.description: Explains why the landmarks are drawn rather than judged.

landmarkOutlineLabel.description: Label above the nested list of landmark regions.

landmarkNoName.description: Shown for a landmark that carries no accessible name.

landmarkNoneFound.message: No landmark regions on this page: nothing marks the header, navigation, main or footer
landmarkNoneFound.description: Neither a landmark element nor a landmark role was found.

landmarkNoMain.message: No main landmark: nothing marks where the page's own content begins
landmarkNoMain.description: A page should have exactly one main region to skip to.

landmarkMultipleMain.message: $count$ main landmarks: only one may be exposed on a page
landmarkMultipleMain.description: More than one main breaks a hard rule; only one main is allowed.

landmarkSameNameless.message: $count$ $role$ landmarks with no name: a screen reader announces them identically
landmarkSameNameless.description: Landmarks of one role, none named, cannot be told apart when jumping between them.

landmarkSameName.message: $count$ $role$ landmarks share the name “$name$”: nothing tells them apart
landmarkSameName.description: Landmarks of one role with the same accessible name are announced identically.

commandLandmarkCheck.description: Keyboard shortcut description for the landmark checker.

copyLandmarks.description: Copy button label for the nested landmark list.

checkNoindex.message: robots contains noindex: this page asks not to be listed in search results
checkNoindex.description: The most consequential thing to find on a production page.

checkNofollow.message: robots contains nofollow: links on this page ask not to be followed
checkNofollow.description: Less severe than noindex but worth surfacing.

checkQuirks.message: No usable doctype: the page is rendering in quirks mode

checkViewportMissing.message: No viewport: the page will not scale on phones

checkCanonicalElsewhere.message: canonical points at another page, so this one asks not to be treated as the original: $url$
checkCanonicalElsewhere.description: Fine when deliberate, serious when not.

checkHreflangNoSelf.message: No hreflang names this page: a set that does not include itself is ignored entirely
checkHreflangNoSelf.description: Only reported when the page declares hreflang at all - a single language site needs none.

checkHreflangInvalid.message: hreflang="$code$" is not a language tag, so that alternate is ignored
checkHreflangInvalid.description: Structural check: the value cannot be parsed as BCP 47.

checkHreflangMistaken.message: hreflang="$code$" is a country code, not a language: this language is "$correct$"
checkHreflangMistaken.description: The common confusion - jp for ja, cn for zh. Only codes that are not languages in their own right are listed.

checkHreflangConflict.message: hreflang="$code$" is given twice with different addresses
checkHreflangConflict.description: Nothing can decide which of the two was meant.

checkDuplicate.description: Duplicated tags leave the outcome up to whoever reads them.

checkTooLong.message: $tag$ is $count$ characters, long enough to be cut off
checkTooLong.description: Length only - whether the text is right is a human question.

checkRefresh.description: A redirect hidden in the markup.

checkOgImageSmall.message: og:image is $width$x$height$, smaller than the 1200x630 most platforms want

checkOgImageFailed.description: The URL is set but nothing came back.

headCharacterCount.description: Length of a metadata value, counted in code points.

headOneOfSeveral.description: Open Graph lets og:image repeat. Only the first is previewed, so the row says how many there were.

valueNotSet.description: Shown when a metadata tag is absent.

valueEmpty.description: Shown when a metadata tag is present but has no value.

altPresent.description: Label showing an image's alt text.

altMissing.description: Label for an image with no alt attribute at all.

altEmpty.description: Label for an image with alt="", i.e. marked decorative.

popupReviewCopied.description: Shown after the popup has put every panel's findings on the clipboard.

popupCannotRun.message: Krafty cannot run on this page.
popupCannotRun.description: Shown in the popup when the page cannot be scripted.

manualTitle.description: Heading and browser tab title of the manual page.

manualHeadBody.message: Report what can be checked automatically, preview how the page appears in search results and when shared, and list every value in the head.

manualNestBody.message: Highlight elements that their parent element is not allowed to contain, explain each one on hover, and count them by kind.

manualHeadingBody.message: Report what can be checked automatically about the heading structure, and list the headings as an indented outline for you to read.

manualMarkupBody.message: Report markup that is wrong in ways the page does not show: ids used more than once, tables with no header cells, form fields and links and buttons with nothing naming them, inline SVGs that are neither named nor hidden, and contradictions in a page's ARIA. Hover a finding to box the element on the page and click to scroll to it.

manualImageBody.message: Find images served much larger than the space they are shown in, and images with no width and height attributes. Allows for high-density displays, so a correctly built retina image is not reported.

manualLeftoversBody.message: Find development leftovers a page shipped with: resources pointing at a local or private address no visitor can reach, http resources on an https page that the browser blocks, and images still coming from a placeholder service.

manualOutlineBody.message: Display an outline around every element.

manualAltBody.message: Display the alt attribute of every image, and flag images whose alt is missing or empty.

manualShortcutsIntro.message: Every checker can be toggled from the keyboard, but none come with a key already assigned. Pick your own here:

manualShortcutsWhy.message: Krafty ships no default keys on purpose. Chrome drops a suggested key that anything else already claims, and does so without an error anywhere, so a default that looks reasonable can simply never work and leave the feature looking broken. Choosing your own takes a moment and always works.
